headings.title: 3× risk gate (applies at every gate in phases 3–8)

sections.title: AGENTS.md — visual motion pipeline
sections.text:
# AGENTS.md — visual motion pipeline

**Status: draft (post-research synthesis).** Research from
`planning/research/01`, `02`, `03` is landed and synthesized in
`planning/20-research-synthesis.md`. Numeric commitments below cite
primary sources. Items still pending are marked `[TODO]`.

Modeled on `~/work/AGENTS.md` and `~/projects/server-manager/AGENTS.md`.
Adapted for the visual-motion pipeline problem space documented in
`planning/00-problem-space.md`.

The pipeline plans, reviews, and researches motion for six mediums
(sprite, model, video, SVG/UI, transitions, presentations), across
multiple content types (fight / narrative / mechanical / ambient /
microinteraction) and multiple UI subtypes (game / app / landing /
email / dashboard / onboarding). It does not replace animators or
designers — it handles planning, review, and research around their
work.

**First-class rule.** Motion is data, not screenshots. Every critique
cites a numeric signal (px/s, jerk, contact distance, phase offset,
contrast, WCAG metric). The temple-brawl exercise (documented in
`workspace/`; source video at `temple-brawl-v1.mp4` in the project
root) is the project's rolling regression test: any pipeline change
must still catch F1–F10 in `planning/00-problem-space.md §6`.

**Second-class rule: numeric thresholds come from policy, never from
inline code or agent procedures.** Every threshold is keyed to
`projects/<project>/policy.md`, with a pipeline-default fallback in
`projects/_template/policy.md`. Scripts and agents read via
`tools.policy.load(...)`. A project that wants different thresholds
overrides the keys; it does not edit agent procedures.

sections.title: What this pipeline is NOT
sections.text:
## What this pipeline is NOT

(Anti-scope, mirrored from `planning/00-problem-space.md §8` so new
sub-document authors see it before scope decisions.)

- Not a replacement for animators or designers. This is planning,
  review, and research tooling around human work.
- Not a single super-pipeline. Multiple pipelines per medium/content,
  sharing only what genuinely overlaps.
- Not a Photoshop clone. Touch-up is Python image tooling, not a GUI.
- No auto-approve of motion fidelity — every phase has a review gate.
- No compatibility shims or transitional phases.
- No automatic grading against rules that lack a citation or a
  declared project policy. Unsourced rules are reported, not graded.

---

sections.title: Tier safety
sections.text:
## Tier safety

Motion work is authored locally, so most actions are Tier 2 (confined
write). A small number are Tier 3:

| Tier | Examples | Authorization |
|---|---|---|
| 1 — Read | Read video, read pose.npz, read project files | Always |
| 2 — Confined write | Generate SVGs, render videos, write review docs | Always |
| 3 — Visible write | Publish to a landing page, deploy to a web app, email-ready HTML, push a PR | Explicit per-action approval |

Tier-3 rule mirrors `~/projects/server-manager/AGENTS.md` — describe
exactly what will happen, wait for explicit yes, no batching.

---

sections.title: Workflows (cue → workflow)
sections.text:
## Workflows (cue → workflow)

| Cue | Workflow |
|---|---|
| "Analyse this reference video" / "What happens at t=X?" | Motion Analysis |
| "Critique this motion" / "Is this choreography good?" | Critique |
| "Are my critique's numbers actually right?" | Review Audit (inside Critique) |
| "Plan a new scene" / "Storyboard X" | Planning |
| "Make this look like that" / "Generate X in style Y" | Generation |
| "Review this UI motion" / "Is this accessible?" | HCI Review |
| "Research X" / "What are the options for Y?" | Research |
| "How should we rank the backlog?" | Roadmap |

If a user's request matches multiple cues, run them in the order above
(analyse → critique → plan → generate → review).

---

sections.title: Motion Analysis workflow
sections.text:
## Motion Analysis workflow

For understanding a reference or a draft. Produces numeric timeline
artefacts other workflows consume.

| Phase | Agent | Artefact | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Probe | Orchestrator | — | duration, FPS, resolution, frame count |
| 2. Flow + pose | `motion-signal-extractor` | `analysis/flow.npz`, `analysis/pose.npz`, `attribution-caveats.md` | Per-frame motion mass + per-frame pose per character |
| 3. Derive | `motion-deriver` | `analysis/summary.npz`, `analysis/smoothness.npz` | v / a / j per joint; LDLJ + SPARC per character |
| 4. Events | `event-detector` | `analysis/events.json` | Strike / kick / dodge / travel / quiet windows with contact bands |
| 5. Timeline | `timeline-writer` | `analysis/TIMELINE.md` | Human-readable beat-by-beat (describe only, no critique) |

Each agent does one verb. Previously these were bundled in a single
`motion-analyst` god agent; split per this AGENTS.md's "no super-
agents" rule.

---

sections.title: Critique workflow
sections.text:
## Critique workflow

| Phase | Agent | Artefact | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Motion analysis | `motion-analyst` | see above | prerequisite |
| 2. Choreography review | `choreography-reviewer` | `reviews/choreography-<date>.md` | numeric critique against principles |
| 3. Composition review | `composition-reviewer` | `reviews/composition-<date>.md` | framing, silhouette, value, eye-line |
| 4. HCI review (UI only) | `hci-reviewer` | `reviews/hci-<date>.md` | Fitts / Hick / WCAG / platform |
| 5. **Review audit** | `review-auditor` | `reviews/audit-<date>.md` | cross-check every numeric claim in phases 2–4 against the actual `.npz`. Catches reviewer factual errors (F4/F5/F6 class). |
| 6. Synthesis | Orchestrator | `reviews/summary-<date>.md` | prioritised findings |

Reviewers **do not propose fixes**. Findings go to the planner for a
revise loop. The review-auditor ensures reviewers' numbers are right
before their findings feed into the revise loop.

---

sections.title: Planning workflow
sections.text:
## Planning workflow

For creating a new motion sequence.

| Phase | Agent / Role | Artefact | Gate |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0. Brief | Human | `brief.md` | Human |
| 1. Reference analysis (if reference) | Motion Analysis workflow | `analysis/TIMELINE.md` | Model |
| 2. Beat sheet | `beat-planner` | `plan/beats.md` | Human — scope check |
| 3. SVG plan (low-fi) | `svg-planner` | `plan/<shot>.svg` | 3× risk |
| 4. Plan review | choreography + composition reviewers | `reviews/*.md` | Human — confirm critiques addressed |
| 5. Storyboard rasterisation | `storyboarder` (multimodal) | `storyboard/<shot>/panel-*.png` | 3× risk |
| 6. Keyframes | `keyframe-artist` | `keyframes/<shot>/k*.png` | 3× risk + medium-specific risk |
| 7. In-betweens | `inbetweener` | `inbetweens/<shot>/f*.png` | 3× risk + smoothness check |
| 8. Composite | `compositor` | `composite/<shot>.png\|.mp4` | Medium-specific + HCI (if UI) |
| 9. Touch-up | `retoucher` | `final/<shot>.*` | Quality gate (multimodal) |
| 10. Final | Human | deliverable | Human approves |

Each phase writes to a phase-specific sub-directory. See §"Artifact
naming" below.

sections.title: 3× risk gate (applies at every gate in phases 3–8)
sections.text:
### 3× risk gate (applies at every gate in phases 3–8)

Three parallel assessors from `agents/risk/`:

- `risk/motion-fidelity.md` — data-grounding, smoothness, timing,
  contact accuracy, phase relationships, continuity
- `risk/composition.md` — silhouette, value, eye-line, depth, density
- `risk/story-beat.md` — phrase structure, rhythm, alignment, stakes

Medium-specific (add one of the below, matched to the medium):

- `risk/sprite.md` — pivot, frame count, loop-seam pop
- `risk/rig.md` — weight paint, IK, bone constraints, foot-skate,
  floor penetration, gimbal lock, bone-parented props
- `risk/video-continuity.md` — identity drift, pop, judder, frame-rate
  mismatch, generative drift
- `risk/ui-motion.md` — RAIL, WCAG 2.2, CLS, platform tokens,
  reduced-motion APIs, subtype-specific (game / landing / email /
  dashboard / onboarding)

All numeric thresholds in the above read from `policy.*` keys; see
`projects/_template/policy.md`.

All must return LOW. If any MEDIUM/HIGH: revise the highest-risk
dimension, re-run the gate. No cherry-picking.

---

sections.title: Generation workflow
sections.text:
## Generation workflow

For producing new visuals (rasterised images, generated video, SVG
animations) rather than planning or reviewing.

| Phase | Tool / Agent | Artefact |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Prompt build | Orchestrator | `generation/<shot>.prompt.md` |
| 2. Generate | `seedream-t2i` / `seedance-t2v` / `gemini-*` / custom SVG generator | raster/video |
| 3. Self-review (multimodal) | `generation-reviewer` | `generation/<shot>.review.md` |
| 4. Regenerate on fail (bounded) | same generator | next iteration |

See `~/.claude/projects/-home-nes-projects-videos/memory/visual_workflow.md`
for the multimodal CLI setup.

---

sections.title: HCI Review workflow (UI work only)
sections.text:
## HCI Review workflow (UI work only)

Uses `agents/hci-reviewer.md`. Cites `planning/research/03-hci-and-review.md`.

| Phase | Agent | What's checked | Primary sources |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Subtype classify | Orchestrator | game / app / landing / email / dashboard / onboarding | — |
| 2. Core heuristics | `hci-reviewer` A1–A6 | Nielsen 10, Shneiderman 8, Fitts, Hick-Hyman, Doherty, RAIL | NN/g 2024; Shneiderman UMD HCIL; Fitts 1954; Hyman 1953; IBM; web.dev |
| 3. WCAG 2.2 | `hci-reviewer` A7 | 2.2.2 Pause/Stop/Hide, 2.3.1 Flashing, 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions, 1.4.3 Contrast | W3C WCAG 2.2 |
| 4. Subtype rules | `hci-reviewer` A8 | game: Desurvire HEP, Pinelle et al., Hodent; app: Apple HIG; landing: NN/g fold, Baymard; email: Can I Email, Litmus; dashboard: Heer & Robertson; onboarding: NN/g auto-forwarding | Research 03 |
| 5. Platform tokens | `hci-reviewer` A9 | Material 3 physics springs (spatial/effects × fast/default/slow), Windows 83/167/250/333 ms, Carbon 70/110/150/240/400/700 ms, Apple qualitative | Platform HIGs |
| 6. Perf + CLS | `risk/ui-motion.md` | RAIL profile, CLS from motion, layout-triggering property detection | web.dev |

Note: Material Design 3 (May 2025) replaced its older duration/easing
ladder with a physics-based system. Some third-party blog posts still
cite the old tokens — this pipeline uses the current physics system
per m3.material.io.

---

sections.title: Research workflow
sections.text:
## Research workflow

For open questions the project doesn't already have answers to.

Based on the `~/projects/server-manager/AGENTS.md` pattern:

| Phase | Agent | Role |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Scope | Orchestrator | Split the question into focused sub-questions |
| 2. Research (parallel) | `gpt-high` × N researchers | Web search, cite primary sources, write to `planning/research/NN-topic.md` |
| 3. Synthesis | Orchestrator | Integrate findings into project context |
| 4. Decision | Human | User decides what to adopt |

**No super-agents.** A researcher gets one focused question; the
orchestrator does the synthesis. Prompt structure in
`planning/research/*.prompt.md` is the reference.

Current outputs:
- `planning/research/01-animation-fundamentals.md` — **landed**
  (Disney 12 × medium, SPARC/LDLJ, failure taxonomy, pose tools,
  SAFD conventions, Bordwell HK measurements)
- `planning/research/02-medium-pipelines.md` — **landed**
  (Aseprite/Spine/DragonBones/Rive, Blender/Maya/Houdini/Rokoko,
  AE/Nuke/AI video, Lottie/WAAPI/Motion Canvas, Material/HIG/Fluent/
  Carbon/Radix, PowerPoint/Keynote/Figma, Mayer/Sweller)
- `planning/research/03-hci-and-review.md` — **landed**
  (Nielsen/Shneiderman/Fitts/Hick-Hyman/Doherty/RAIL/WCAG 2.2, game
  UX heuristics, landing NN/g, email constraints, Apple/Material/
  Fluent/Windows/Carbon tokens, Heer & Robertson, X-sheet, Pixar
  dailies, Valve playtesting)

Synthesis: `planning/20-research-synthesis.md`.

---

sections.title: Roadmap workflow
sections.text:
## Roadmap workflow

Layered, matching `~/projects/server-manager/AGENTS.md` roadmap loop:

| Layer | Output | Risk types |
|---|---|---|
| 0. Market research | target-audience & competitor visual standards | market misread, dependency, completeness |
| 1. Executive roadmap | strategic ordering of mediums / content / UI types | market misread, dependency, completeness |
| 2. Engineering roadmap | pipeline + tooling ordering | feasibility, integration, drift |
| 3. AI roadmap | which agents are worth building, in what order | decomposition, coverage, dependency |
| 4. Tickets | individual agent / tool implementations | — |

The roadmap loop is not needed for the initial pipeline build — it
applies to expanding the pipeline with new mediums / content types over
time.

---

sections.title: Gate ownership
sections.text:
## Gate ownership

| Phase | Gate owner | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Problem space | Human | Scope / framing |
| Research | Human after each researcher report | Confirm findings are relevant |
| Synthesis | Human | Needs mapping |
| Proposal (pipeline design) | 3× `claude-opus` parallel risk | Risk IS the review |
| Hookpoint (what code/tools to reuse) | Human | Confirm pre-existing survives |
| Per-artefact plan / review (during project work) | 3× risk | Model |
| Final deliverable | Human | User approves before ship |

Rule: phases that set **scope** belong to the user; phases that
**validate design or output** belong to model gates.

---

sections.title: Model roles
sections.text:
## Model roles

Mirrors `~/work/AGENTS.md` and `~/projects/server-manager/AGENTS.md`:

| Model | Role |
|---|---|
| `gpt-high` | RCA, proposals, research, hookpoint analysis, implementation |
| `gpt-xhigh` | Coordinator for large tasks |
| `claude-opus` | Risk assessments, reviews, alignment, synthesis |
| `claude-sonnet` | Quick checks, summaries |
| `gemini-video-*` | Multimodal video analysis |
| `seedream-*` / `seedance-*` | Image & video generation |

**No super-agents** — coordinator/researcher split on anything larger
than a single focused question.

---

sections.title: Artifact naming
sections.text:
## Artifact naming

Per-project folder structure:

```
projects/videos/projects/
  _template/
    policy.md           # pipeline-default thresholds (schema + fallbacks)
  <project-name>/
    policy.md           # project-specific overrides of _template/policy.md
    brief.md
    reference/
      <ref-video>.mp4
      analysis/
        flow.npz
        pose.npz
        summary.npz
        smoothness.npz
        events.json
        TIMELINE.md
    plan/
      beats.md
      <shot>.svg
      <shot>.mp4        # SVG rendered for review
      reviews/
        choreography-<YYYYMMDD>.md
        composition-<YYYYMMDD>.md
        hci-<YYYYMMDD>.md
        audit-<YYYYMMDD>.md     # review-auditor output — cross-checks the above
    storyboard/
      <shot>/
        panel-<n>.png
    keyframes/
      <shot>/
        k<n>.png
    inbetweens/
      <shot>/
        f<n>.png
    composite/
      <shot>.png|.mp4
    final/
      <shot>.mp4|.gif|.html|.pptx
    risk/
      motion-<YYYYMMDD>.md
      composition-<YYYYMMDD>.md
      story-<YYYYMMDD>.md
      <medium-specific>-<YYYYMMDD>.md
```

**Every project has a `policy.md`.** It overrides `_template/policy.md`
per-key. Scripts and agents load via `tools.policy.load(path)`.
No numeric threshold lives inline in an agent procedure.

Prompts, intermediate `.prompt.md`, and reviews are gitignored.

---

sections.title: Global constraints
sections.text:
## Global constraints

- **Motion is data.** Numeric signals are authoritative; still frames
  are supporting evidence only.
- **No super-agents.** Coordinator / researcher split when a question
  spans multiple topics.
- **No compatibility shims.** Pipelines ship clean.
- **Per-medium risk is additive.** UI work = base three risks + UI
  motion risk. Sprite work = base three + sprite risk. Etc.
- **Human at the endpoints.** Brief + final deliverable are human-gated.
  Middle is model-gated by risk.
- **Prefers-reduced-motion, always.** Any UI motion has a reduced-motion
  fallback planned, not bolted on.
- **Tier-3 rule.** Per-action explicit yes before publishing anything
  user-visible.
- **Regression test.** Pipeline changes run against
  `temple-brawl-v1.mp4` (in the project root). Every risk must still catch its F1–F10
  failure.

---

sections.title: Sub-AGENTS.md documents `[PLANNED]`
sections.text:
## Sub-AGENTS.md documents `[PLANNED]`

For depth without overwhelming the top-level file, medium- and
content-specific rules live in sub-documents:

```
agents/
  AGENTS.sprite.md          # per-medium
  AGENTS.model.md
  AGENTS.video.md
  AGENTS.svg-ui.md
  AGENTS.transition.md
  AGENTS.presentation.md
  AGENTS.content.fight.md   # per-content
  AGENTS.content.narrative.md
  AGENTS.content.mechanical.md
  AGENTS.content.ambient.md
  AGENTS.content.microinteraction.md
  AGENTS.ui.game.md         # per-UI-subtype
  AGENTS.ui.app.md
  AGENTS.ui.landing.md
  AGENTS.ui.email.md
  AGENTS.ui.dashboard.md
  AGENTS.ui.onboarding.md
```

All five content types from the problem space now have a planned
home (previously `mechanical` and `ambient` were missing — corrected
after scope risk assessment).

**`transition` and `presentation` mediums use `risk/ui-motion.md`
when the target is a UI surface (web/app/email/dashboard) and
`risk/composition.md` + `risk/story-beat.md` when they are
non-UI (film scene transition, rendered keynote). This is
deliberate — not a missing medium-specific risk — because UI-shaped
transitions share the RAIL / WCAG / CLS budget while non-UI
transitions share the composition-and-beat concerns. Sub-AGENTS.md
per medium documents the split explicitly.**

Each sub-document follows the same shape: scope, non-negotiables, risk
classes specific to it, reviewer checklist, references. Shared patterns
stay in this top-level file.

---

sections.title: Adding an agent file
sections.text:
## Adding an agent file

New agents go in `agents/*.md` following the `~/work/agents/*.md` format:

```markdown
---
description: 'One-line description'
model: <default-model>
output_format: ''
---

# Agent name

## Use When
## Do Not Use When
## Non-Negotiables
## Required Inputs
## Procedure
## Output Format
## Stop Conditions
```

---

sections.title: Open items
sections.text:
## Open items

Done:
- Research landed (01, 02, 03)
- Synthesis doc (`planning/20-research-synthesis.md`)
- **15 single-concern agents** in `agents/`:
  - motion pipeline: `motion-signal-extractor`, `motion-deriver`,
    `event-detector`, `timeline-writer` (split from the former
    `motion-analyst` god agent)
  - planning: `beat-planner`, `svg-planner`, `storyboarder`,
    `keyframe-artist`, `inbetweener`, `compositor`, `retoucher`
  - review: `choreography-reviewer` (narrowed to narrative/rhythm),
    `composition-reviewer`, `hci-reviewer`, `review-auditor`
- **7 risk assessors** in `agents/risk/`:
  `motion-fidelity`, `composition`, `story-beat`, `ui-motion`,
  `video-continuity`, `rig`, `sprite`
- **17 sub-AGENTS.md craft-rules docs**:
  - `agents/medium/` (6): sprite, model, video, svg-ui, transition,
    presentation
  - `agents/content/` (5): fight, narrative, mechanical, ambient,
    microinteraction
  - `agents/ui/` (6): game, app, landing, email, dashboard,
    onboarding
- Policy infrastructure: `projects/_template/policy.md`,
  `tools/policy.py` loader, `projects/temple-brawl-regression/policy.md`
- Self-test harness skeleton: `tools/run_regression.py`
- Two rounds of 3× risk assessment: round 2 scope LOW; audit +
  shortcut MEDIUM with specific cleanup items, all subsequently
  addressed in follow-up edits.

To do:
- Run round-3 risk re-gate against the post-cleanup state (all
  round-2 blockers visibly addressed by inspection; formal
  verification is a 10-min re-run).
- Wire agent invocations in `tools/run_regression.py` (currently
  dry-run only).
- Self-test: execute `run_regression.py` against the
  `temple-brawl-regression` project and confirm F1–F10 each trigger
  the expected risk dimension.
- Migrate `tools/extract_motion.py` FPS read to
  `tools.policy.load()` (implementation detail; not gate-blocking).

Ordering of the to-do: sub-AGENTS.md per medium + per UI subtype
matter most, since they're where medium-specific craft rules live.
Remaining risk assessors (sprite, rig, video-continuity) come next.
Planning agents (svg-planner, storyboarder, etc.) last — they're
authored artefacts and can share one template.
